expectations.text: Uses len(relation) to count without fetching all rows
expectations.evidence: Code: `len(Electrode & {...})`; explains 'len(<relation>) issues a SELECT COUNT(*) against the database — it does not pull all rows into Python'.

expectations.text: Does not fetch the full Electrode set just to take its length
expectations.evidence: Explicitly flags `len((...).fetch())` as the anti-pattern with 'DON'T — fetches all rows then counts in Python'.

expectations.text: Bonus: notes that each Electrode row is one recording contact (keyed by electrode_id within an ElectrodeGroup), so the count returns total contacts — not tetrodes/shanks/ElectrodeGroups (e.g., a 32-tetrode session yields 128 Electrode rows, not 32)
expectations.evidence: States 'one row per recording contact ... not the number of tetrodes (one tetrode = one ElectrodeGroup with 4 contacts)' and gives 32 ElectrodeGroups → 128 Electrode rows.
